Solum 02 — Powder

Solum
Muscle

Sarcopenia Defense & Muscle Preservation

Format
Powder — 1 scoop daily
Timing
Post-workout or with breakfast
Supply
30 servings
With Fat
Take with dietary fat — D3 & K2 are fat-soluble
Retail
$75
The Clinical Case

Muscle is the longevity organ. Not cardiovascular fitness. Not metabolic markers. Muscle mass and strength are the single most reliable predictors of how long you will function well.

Sarcopenia — age-related muscle loss — begins in your thirties and accelerates sharply after fifty. By seventy, most people have lost 20–40% of their peak muscle mass. The consequences are functional: strength, balance, fall risk, and independence.

These five ingredients address sarcopenia through every mechanism — synthesis, breakdown prevention, ATP production, and vitamin-driven muscle receptor expression — all at clinical doses. Powder format because 11g of actives cannot fit in a practical capsule.

Ingredient Breakdown
Ingredient Dose Clinical Rationale
Creatine MonohydrateCreapure®
 5,000 mg The most researched ingredient in sports nutrition — hundreds of RCTs. 5g daily achieves full phosphocreatine saturation within 28 days. Supports ATP regeneration and directly counters age-related decline in muscle force. Creapure® (AlzChem, Germany) is the only creatine with consistent independent purity verification.
Calcium HMBmyHMB™
 3,000 mg Exact dose from the Wilson et al. and Nissen et al. sarcopenia studies. HMB reduces muscle protein breakdown — the prevention side of muscle protein turnover. Leucine works the synthesis side. Together they cover the full equation. 3g is non-negotiable; lower doses show attenuated effects.
L-LeucineInstantized
 3,000 mg The primary mTOR-activating amino acid. Norton and Layman's research established 2,500–3,000mg per serving is required to maximally trigger muscle protein synthesis. Instantized form with sunflower lecithin coating for clean dispersibility and taste — raw L-Leucine is hydrophobic and bitter.
Vitamin D3CWD Beadlet
 2,000 IU Vitamin D receptor expression in muscle tissue directly regulates muscle protein synthesis — a specific sarcopenia mechanism. Cold-water dispersible beadlet form (Quali-D, DSM) ensures absorption even without a fat-containing meal. Deficiency present in ~40% of US adults.
Vitamin K2 MK-7MenaQ7® CWD
 100 mcg Essential cofactor for D3. Activates Matrix Gla Protein which directs calcium into bone and prevents arterial calcification. Without K2, calcium mobilized by D3 can deposit in arteries. MenaQ7® Matrix CWD form — 99% trans-isomer, cold-water dispersible.
⚠ Required Physician Consultation

Vitamin K2 directly interacts with Warfarin and other anticoagulant medications — do not take without physician oversight if on anticoagulation therapy. Consult your physician before use if you have kidney disease (creatine) or sarcoidosis (Vitamin D3). Note: creatine commonly causes a temporary 1–3 lb weight increase from intramuscular water retention in the first two weeks. This is normal and expected.

Drug Interaction Reference
Vitamin K2 + Warfarin / Anticoagulants
K2 directly opposes Warfarin's mechanism. Anyone on anticoagulation therapy must not take this product without physician oversight. Most critical interaction in the formula.
Vitamin D3 + Thiazide Diuretics
Combination can elevate blood calcium. Not a concern at 2,000 IU in otherwise healthy adults, but monitor if on thiazide diuretics.
Creatine + Caffeine
Early research suggested interference; not replicated in more recent literature. No established clinical concern.
L-Leucine + Insulin Therapy (Type 1 Diabetes)
Leucine stimulates insulin secretion. Relevant for Type 1 diabetics carefully managing blood glucose. Monitor and adjust if needed.

These statements have not been evaluated by the FDA. This product is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease.
Consult your physician before beginning any supplement regimen, especially if taking prescription medications.
